articles.title: FOMC Projections Materials — June 17, 2026
articles.extracted_text: FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75%. Key SEP projections: 2026 PCE inflation revised to 3.6% (from 2.7% in March, +0.9pp). Core PCE 2026: 3.3% (from 2.7%). 2026 GDP: 2.2% (from 2.4%). Unemployment: 4.3% (from 4.4%). Federal funds rate median 2026: 3.8% (from 3.4%), implying at least one 25bp hike. 9 of 18 officials project a rate hike in 2026 (6 project multiple hikes). Inflation uncertainty assessed as 'higher' by 17 of 18 participants. GDP uncertainty split: 9 'broadly similar', 9 'higher'. Risks to inflation weighted to upside by 16 participants.

articles.title: Fed rate decision live: US central bank does not cut rates in first meeting chaired by Warsh
articles.extracted_text: Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% in Warsh's first meeting as Chair. Warsh dropped forward guidance entirely: 'not well suited to the current economic moment.' Statement described as 'shorter, simpler' dispensing with 'older language.' Warsh: 'the commitment to deliver is strong, unanimous and unambiguous.' Nine officials anticipate a rate hike by end of 2026. Warsh did not submit a dot plot projection, calling it 'not helpful in the conduct of policy.' Five task forces announced: Communications, Balance Sheet, Data Sources, Productivity/Jobs/AI, Inflation Frameworks. Rate futures now pricing higher probability of September hike than hold. DXY extended gains, euro -0.5% at $1.1553, yen flat at 160.435. Karl Schamotta (Corpay): 'short, but not sweet...committee turned sharply hawkish.' Mark Hackett (Nationwide): 'incrementally hawkish with a less detailed statement.'

articles.title: Fed meeting today: Live updates — Kevin Warsh's First Decision as Chair
articles.extracted_text: Warsh abstained from dot plot submission. Median federal funds rate ends 2026 at 3.8% (up from 3.4% in March), a quarter-point above current range. Market pricing shifted hike expectation from December to October (60.7% probability via CME FedWatch). 2-year Treasury yield jumped ~11 bps to 4.153%; 10-year +4 bps to 4.469%. S&P 500 fell as much as 0.88% intraday. CPI (May): 4.2% annually; core PCE (April): 3.8%. Goldman Sachs (Kay Haigh): base case still no hikes but 'path is narrow.' Citi: first meetings of new chairs historically see hawkish bias (avg 2Y sell-off of 6 bps). Deutsche Bank: 11 hawkish FOMC speeches since April, 5 neutral, 1 dovish. Warsh confirmed weekly breakfasts with Treasury Secretary Bessent. Powell remains as Fed governor (first ex-chair as governor since 1951).

articles.title: Markets News Today: Stocks Slump on Fed Rate Decision; SpaceX Stock on Track to Snap Winning Streak
articles.extracted_text: Dow -0.8%, Nasdaq -0.9%, S&P 500 -1%. Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75%. Dot plot: 9 of 18 FOMC members anticipate at least one rate hike this year (zero in March). Warsh: 'wouldn't be particularly intrigued' by market reactions. SOX +3% after Tuesday slump. AVGO +5% after JPMorgan urged buyers to 'ignore the noise' on Google chip delay rumors; AI revenue to double YoY in 2027 and 2028. Citigroup: chipmakers to spend $250B on wafer fab equipment in 2028 (+25% from 2027). AMAT +7%, LRCX +5%, KLAC +3%. SpaceX (SPCX) -2% to ~$199 (touched $187 intraday), market cap ~$2.7T. IPO price $135, up ~50% in first three sessions. Cursor acquisition: $60B all-stock deal. ACN preview: Thursday pre-market, options imply ±7% move, YTD -40%. JBL Q3 beat: $8.75B revenue (+12% YoY), EPS $2.59, raised FY guidance to $35B revenue, EPS $12.70. CME -2.8% after CEO Duffy announced March 2027 departure. Oil: WTI $76.20, Brent $79. 10Y: 4.49% (+4 bps). Bitcoin: $64,600, Gold: $4,260 (-2%). DXY: 100.40 (+0.8%).

articles.title: Stock Market Today (June 17, 2026): S&P 500, Nasdaq plummet as Fed meeting points to rate hike in late 2026
articles.extracted_text: S&P 500 -1.21%, Nasdaq -1.34%, Dow -0.97%, Russell 2000 -0.74%. VIX +12% to 18.44. 10Y yield +6.9 bps to 4.497%. Selloff intensified in final minutes after Warsh press conference. Dot plot: 9 of 18 project at least one hike, 6 project multiple hikes. Warsh: 'I can't give you any guidance on what we're going to do next.' 'Fed policy appears to be restrictive vis a vis the housing market, but not financial markets.' Pre-Fed: markets pricing 97% probability of no change. BofA survey: 55% expected hawkish Warsh tone. Iran peace deal: MoU signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening, Friday Geneva signing, $300B+ reconstruction for Iran. Trump warned deal 'not final' and threatened to 'go right back to dropping bombs.' SPR: largest-ever draw of 8.263M barrels, decades-low stockpiles. Israel continued Lebanon strikes. AMAT +9.3%, LRCX +6.6%, ARM +6.2% intraday. SpaceX -2.9% to $196. BMW -7% on China demand + Iran war profit warning. Bitcoin -2.37% to $64,795.

articles.title: United States Crude Oil Inventories — June 17, 2026
articles.extracted_text: EIA Crude Oil Inventories (June 17, 2026): Actual -8.263M barrels (draw), Forecast -3.600M, Previous -7.227M. Largest-ever Strategic Petroleum Reserve draw reported, bringing stockpiles to decades-low operational stress levels. WTI crude: $76.27/bbl (+0.29%), Brent: $79.24 (+0.35%). Prior day saw ~4% decline on Iran peace deal expectations.

articles.title: Iran will reopen the Strait of Hormuz and can sell oil freely under new deal
articles.extracted_text: U.S. and Iran reached framework agreement to end three-month conflict. Strait of Hormuz to reopen under agreement. Iran allowed to resume oil exports without restrictions. Formal signing set for Friday in Geneva. Iran to receive at least $300B for reconstruction. Trump warned deal 'not final,' threatened to resume bombing if unsatisfied. Israel continued strikes in Lebanon, Iran warning this violates deal terms. Shipping firms remain cautious amid naval blockade uncertainty.

articles.title: Broadcom Stock Is Down in June. J.P. Morgan Says Would Be Aggressive Buyers
articles.extracted_text: JPMorgan: 'Ignore the noise' on Google TPU delay rumors, aggressive buyers at current levels. Broadcom management expects AI revenue in H2 to double from H1. JPMorgan price target: $580 (54% upside). AI revenue expected to hit $16B in July quarter, +200% YoY. AI revenue projected to double YoY in both 2027 and 2028. Custom-chip dominance and execution track record cited. AVGO +5.17% on the session.

articles.title: Chip Stocks Rebound: Semi-Cap Equipment Makers Surge on Citi Bull Call
articles.extracted_text: Citigroup forecast: chipmakers to spend $250B on wafer fab equipment in 2028 (+25% from 2027). Price targets raised ~30-40% on AMAT, LRCX, KLAC. AMAT +7%, LRCX +5%, KLAC +3%. SOX index +3% on the session, recovering from correction territory earlier in June. Context: profit-taking ahead of SpaceX IPO had pushed SOX into decline.

articles.title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Startup Cursor in $60B Deal; Stock Cools After Record Run
articles.extracted_text: SpaceX acquired AI coding startup Cursor in $60B all-stock deal, pushing valuation up 20% to record. SPCX fell 2.9% to $196, first full-day drop after IPO (priced at $135 June 12). Touched $187 intraday, prior session high $226.82. Market cap ~$2.7T (5th largest globally, between Amazon $2.65T and Microsoft $3T). Vanda Track: 'SpaceX is fast becoming a retail darling.' FAB 10: Mag 7 + SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic. Analysts note SpaceX now seen as AI player, not just a rocket company. Expected index inclusion to trigger institutional buying.

articles.title: Jabil Q3 Beat: Revenue $8.75B, Raises Full-Year Guidance on AI Infrastructure Demand
articles.extracted_text: Jabil Q3 FY2026: Revenue $8.75B (+12% YoY), EPS $2.59. CEO: 'AI infrastructure demand remains extremely strong.' Raised FY guidance: Revenue to $35B (from $34B), core EPS to $12.70 (from $12.25). Stock +7% on the session (intraday high +13%).

articles.title: Accenture Earnings Preview: Q3 FY2026 — Options Imply ±7% Move
articles.extracted_text: Accenture reports Q3 FY2026 Thursday pre-market. Options imply ±7% move ($154-$177 range). YTD decline: -40% on AI disruption fears. Expected: Revenue $18.8B (+6% YoY), adjusted EPS $3.72. Goldman Sachs: investors remain 'negatively positioned.'

articles.title: US High Yield Master II Option-Adjusted Spread — June 17, 2026
articles.extracted_text: US High Yield Master II OAS: 2.71% (vs 2.66% prior day, +5 bp). Investment grade OAS: ~0.73% (via StreetStats, June 15). IG corporate yields: 5.14%. HY corporate yields: 6.84%. Spread widening modest despite equity selloff, suggesting credit markets less panicked than equities.

articles.title: Wells Fargo Raises S&P 500 Year-End Target to 7950 on AI and Peace Deal
articles.extracted_text: Wall Street targets: Wells Fargo raised S&P 500 year-end target to 7950 (from 7300), citing Iran peace deal easing macro risks and AI upside. Evercore ISI: S&P 500 could hit 9000, SpaceX IPO to kickstart 'next leg of the bull market.' Wedbush: IPO is 'Goldilocks outcome' for tech. UBS: target 7900, warns White House restrictions on Anthropic models could 'weaken investor confidence in semiconductor demand.' S&P 500 currently at 7420 after Wednesday's selloff — implies +7.1% to Wells target, +21.3% to Evercore target.

articles.title: CME Group CEO Terry Duffy to Step Down in March 2027; Lynne Fitzpatrick Named Successor
articles.extracted_text: CME Group CEO Terry Duffy to step down in March 2027 after 25+ years leading the company. Lynne Fitzpatrick named successor. CME shares fell 2.8% on the announcement.

articles.title: Super Micro Issues Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock Offering
articles.extracted_text: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) issued mandatory convertible preferred stock offering. Stock trading at $29.14, -0.27% on June 17. Top investor quoted: 'It's just not clean.' SMCI's AI ambitions sparked brutal selloff on June 13. Company launched major equity financing initiative June 12. SMCI growing fast but market still worried.
